verdict_reason: Both Shankara and Ramanuja read 2.44 as the consequence-clause of 2.42-43; both read it as a structural-causal chain (not a moral judgment). They differ slightly on what samādhi means here — Shankara: the collected mental state in which Knowledge can be received; Ramanuja: explicitly 'a calmed mind'. The engineering layer preserves both readings parenthetically: 'samādhi-state (Shankara: collected mind ready for Knowledge; Ramanuja: calmed mind)'. Apahṛta-cetas, bhoga-aiśvarya-prasakti, vyavasāyātmikā buddhi, samādhi all preserved as load-bearing transliterated terms. The constitutive force of the chain (attachment captures cetas, capture blocks samādhi, no samādhi means no resolved buddhi) is preserved exactly as both commentators read it. PASS for both 7.4 and 6.8.
